personas.name: Julia Anderson
personas.role: London office; reports missing £2,500 and requests an incident via Employee Agent

personas.name: Jason
personas.role: Resolves UK regional payroll cases in the fulfiller console (Slack channel); approves batch off-cycle corrections

personas.name: Slackbot
personas.role: Employee Agent: payroll intake, Workday/ADP audit, proactive fulfiller alerts, multi-channel pattern correlation

content.text: Hey, I just checked my payslip and it looks like I'm missing £2,500. Can you log an incident for me?

content.statusText: Parsing payroll discrepancy, pulling your profile, and opening a UK regional case…

content.text:
Hi Julia—I've handled the intake side for you.

*What I picked up*
• Intent: *Payroll discrepancy* (£2,500 short on your latest payslip)
• Your employee ID came straight from your Slack profile—no extra forms needed

*Case*
• *#8842* — priority *High*
• Routed to the *UK Regional* fulfiller queue
• I've already kicked off a Workday ↔ ADP cross-check so HR isn't starting from zero

You'll get another DM from me once there's a payment date or if I need one quick clarification. Thanks for flagging it early—that helps us protect everyone on the same payroll lane.

content.statusText: Syncing Case #8842 with your fulfiller console view…

content.text: New assignment: payroll discrepancy escalated from a single employee report (London).

caseFields.label: Source thread
caseFields.value: Slack DM with Employee Agent (full transcript linked in console)

caseFields.label: AI triage note
caseFields.value: Automated audit running: Workday compensation vs ADP pay calc

content.caseNote: Jason—context from Julia's DM is attached so you can see exactly what she saw on her payslip.

content.statusText: Comparing Workday base, allowances, and effective dates against ADP for this cycle…

content.text:
Jason—I've noticed a structural mismatch worth your eyes before you touch adjustments.

*Proactive Assistance* (high signal)
• *Red alert:* Employee received a *4.5% raise* in HRIS effective *Jan 1*; payroll is still calculating at the *2023* rate.
• That explains Julia's £2,500 gap on this run—it's not a one-off keying error on her record alone.

*What you can do*
• Open the side-by-side breakdown to confirm comp rows and effective dating before we widen the blast radius.

Choose an action below to continue.

Here's the clean split, Jason—same story in both systems, different effective dates.

*Workday (HRIS)*
• Base salary band updated *Jan 1* (+4.5%)
• Approval chain complete; no pending workflow on Julia's profile

*ADP (payroll engine)*
• Pay calc still referencing *2023* rate table for this EMEA lane
• Allowance and tax wrappers synced; *base rate* is the stuck field

*So what*
• Julia's £2,500 delta is consistent with the raise row not flowing into ADP's calc file for February.
• Next I'm scanning *Slack*, *email*, and the *HR portal* from the last *2 hours* to see who's else is hitting the same pattern—stand by for volume.

content.statusText: Correlating new tickets across Slack, email, and portal (last 2 hours)…

content.text:
I've handled the pattern pass—Julia wasn't the only one caught in the drift.

*Correlation (last 2 hours)*
• *76* open or reopened items with the same *Jan 1 raise / 2023 payroll rate* signature
• Channels: Slack DMs, payroll inbox, and self-service portal—merged into one cohort for you
• Estimated combined variance: *£190,000* across the group (same root cause as Case #8842)

*Recommended path*
• One regional reconciler run → single off-cycle file to ADP → all 76 corrected together

You can kick the batch fix when you're ready—I'll post confirmations back to each thread.

Choose an action below to continue.

content.statusText: Staging OC-UK-8842-BATCH, linking Case #8842, and submitting to ADP…

content.text:
Nice work, Jason—I've handled the submission side.

*Batch closure*
• ADP accepted *OC-UK-8842-BATCH*
• *76* employees queued on the same off-cycle pay run (Julia included)
• Case *#8842* is the anchor record for audit; the rest are cross-linked

Payroll notifications are going out in Slack so no one's left guessing. If ADP bounces a row, I'll ping you here with the exact exception file.

Julia—quick, honest update on your £2,500.

*What happened*
• Your raise hit Workday *Jan 1*, but our payroll engine was still pricing you at *2023* rates—classic sync lag, not something you did wrong.
• Your report was the nudge that let us catch *75 colleagues* in the same situation.

*What's next*
• HR approved a single off-cycle correction; you're in that batch with everyone else
• Funds should follow your payroll calendar's next off-cycle date—I'll DM again if that window moves

Thanks again—you saved a lot of people a slower, lonelier fight with payroll.
